description: GameData defines a structured configuration for resolving and manipulating client/server memory. It supports addresses, signatures, patches, and vtable mappings across multiple platforms for runtime introspection and modification.
title: GameConfig

addressObject.description: Represents a single step in an address resolution chain used for memory navigation. Each step transforms or dereferences the current pointer to reach a target location.

offset.description: Adds a fixed byte offset to the current address pointer.

index.description: Indexes into an array or structured memory layout relative to the current address.

read.description: Dereferences the current pointer once and applies an additional offset to the resulting address.

read_offs32.description: Performs a 32-bit relative pointer read, commonly used for RIP-relative addressing or position-independent memory layouts.

signatureObject.description: Represents a single signature resolution step used in pattern-based memory scanning. Each field defines a different method of locating or resolving a target symbol.

string.description: Matches a raw string pattern against memory or module contents during signature scanning.

cvar.description: Resolves a console variable (cvar) used for engine-level configuration or runtime state lookup.

vtable.description: Resolves a target via virtual function table (vtable) lookup for class or interface-based dispatch structures.

^(?:(?!\\$).*)$.description: Game configuration entry grouped by game or application name. Contains memory resolution definitions including addresses, offsets, signatures, patches, and vtables.

Addresses.description: Collection of named memory addresses resolved via direct references, signatures, or vtables.

^.+$.description: Named address entry defining how a memory address is resolved.

address.description: Direct reference name to another address entry in the Addresses section.

signature.description: Reference to a signature entry used to locate this address via pattern scanning.

vtable.description: Reference to a vtable entry used to resolve this address through virtual dispatch structures.

win64.description: Platform-specific address resolution steps for Windows x64.

linuxsteamrt64.description: Platform-specific address resolution steps for Linux Steam Runtime x64.

osx64.description: Platform-specific address resolution steps for macOS x64.

Offsets.description: Collection of named numeric offsets used for platform-specific memory calculations.

^.+$.description: Named offset entry containing platform-specific offset values.

win64.description: Offset value for Windows x64 platform.

linuxsteamrt64.description: Offset value for Linux Steam Runtime x64 platform.

osx64.description: Offset value for macOS x64 platform.

Signatures.description: Collection of named signatures used for pattern-based memory scanning and symbol resolution.

^.+$.description: Named signature entry defining how a target symbol is located in memory.

library.description: Name of the shared library or module where the signature is searched.

win64.description: Signature resolution steps for Windows x64 platform.

linuxsteamrt64.description: Signature resolution steps for Linux Steam Runtime x64 platform.

osx64.description: Signature resolution steps for macOS x64 platform.

Patches.description: Collection of named memory patches applied to modify executable code or data at runtime.

^.+$.description: Named patch entry defining target location and platform-specific patch data.

address.description: Reference to an address entry used as patch target.

signature.description: Reference to a signature entry used to locate patch target dynamically.

vtable.description: Reference to a vtable entry used to resolve patch target.

win64.description: Patch byte string for Windows x64. '?' represents a wildcard byte.

linuxsteamrt64.description: Patch byte string for Linux Steam Runtime x64. '?' represents a wildcard byte.

osx64.description: Patch byte string for macOS x64. '?' represents a wildcard byte.

VTables.description: Collection of virtual function table definitions used for class and interface resolution.

^.+$.description: Named vtable entry mapping a class/interface to its binary location.

library.description: Name of the shared library containing the vtable.

table.description: Name or identifier of the virtual function table.
